info_gaps: neurovascular status of foot (cap refill, distal pulses, sensation)
Ottawa ankle rule exam findings (bony tenderness at malleoli/navicular/5th MT)

resource.note: 4 of 12 general beds free — no resource pressure for low-acuity ortho
1 monitored bed free — reserve for higher-acuity queue (3 'now' tier patients waiting)

time.note: Current wait 18 min; acceptable for ESI 4
3 'now' tier patients ahead — this case should not jump queue

warnings: Do not displace higher-acuity patients for ankle x-ray; fast-track or minor-care pathway preferred

hypotheses.name: Lateral malleolus / distal fibula fracture
rules_against: Ottawa criteria not yet documented as positive

hypotheses.name: 5th metatarsal avulsion fracture
rules_against: nurse note localizes swelling to lateral ankle, not midfoot

monitor.action: Apply RICE (rest, ice, compression, elevation) and offer PO analgesia (acetaminophen or ibuprofen) while awaiting room
monitor.reason: Comfort measures and pain control appropriate for low-acuity musculoskeletal injury during wait.

monitor.action: Apply Ottawa Ankle Rules to determine if x-ray (single resource) is needed
monitor.reason: Decision rule determines whether this is ESI 4 (1 resource) vs ESI 5 (no resource); affects pathway.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Redirect to fast-track / minor-care area when room available
escalate_or_redirect.reason: Stable isolated extremity injury; ED main beds should be preserved for the 3 'now'-tier patients in queue.

explanation.summary: 17yo healthy female with isolated right lateral ankle injury, partial weight-bearing, normal vitals, pain 5/10. No red flags. Likely needs ≤1 resource (ankle x-ray if Ottawa-positive). Appropriate for ESI 4 / 'wait' tier and fast-track disposition. Three higher-acuity 'now' patients are queued — this case should not preempt them.

citations.signal: stable vitals, likely single resource (ankle x-ray) per Ottawa rules
alternative if Ottawa-negative — clinical exam + wrap, no imaging required

checklist.task: Apply Ottawa Ankle Rules; document bony tenderness at posterior malleoli, navicular, base of 5th MT, and weight-bearing ability (4 steps)
Provide ice pack, elevate limb, offer PO ibuprofen 400mg or acetaminophen 650mg (no allergies on file)
If Ottawa-positive → order 3-view ankle x-ray; if negative → clinical exam by MD and discharge planning
Reassess pain score and neurovascular status (distal pulses, cap refill, sensation) before disposition
Discharge with RICE instructions, crutches PRN, return precautions, and PCP/sports-med follow-up

why_this_tier: Patient bearing partial weight (reassuring against unstable fracture)
No head/spine involvement, no other injury reported
would_upgrade_if: Inability to bear any weight or gross deformity discovered on exam
Neurovascular compromise (absent pulse, cool foot, paresthesia)
Pain escalates to ≥7/10 or vitals become abnormal
Mechanism reveals higher-energy trauma (e.g., concurrent head strike)
would_downgrade_if: Ottawa Ankle Rules negative AND exam fully reassuring → could be ESI 5 (no resource, simple wrap and discharge)
